name: phi_eigen
purpose: Eigenvalue/eigenvector decomposition with φ-scaled coherence metric for harmonic analysis.
description: Computes the eigendecomposition of a square matrix and adds a φ-coherence metric that measures how 'golden' the eigenvalue spectrum is. Higher coherence indicates eigenvalues are closer together relative to φ.

candid.signature: phiEigen : (vec vec float64) -> (record { values: vec float64; vectors: vec vec float64; phi_coherence: float64 })

vectors.description: Eigenvectors as rows (normalized to unit length)

phi_coherence.interpretation: Higher = eigenvalues closer together, more 'coherent' spectrum

numeric_caveats.description: Eigenvectors are defined up to a sign. v and -v are both valid eigenvectors for the same eigenvalue.
numeric_caveats.mitigation: Compare eigenvectors by computing |v₁ · v₂| ≈ 1 rather than v₁ ≈ v₂

numeric_caveats.description: Non-symmetric matrices may have complex eigenvalues. This function returns only real parts.
numeric_caveats.mitigation: Use symmetric matrices for real eigenvalue guarantee, or check imaginary parts in Julia

numeric_caveats.description: Eigenvalue computation has ~1e-15 relative error for well-conditioned matrices.
numeric_caveats.mitigation: Use tolerance-based comparisons: |λ₁ - λ₂| < ε × max(|λ₁|, |λ₂|)

ai_usage.when_to_use: When you need to understand the fundamental modes/directions of a linear transformation, or when analyzing the spectral properties of a system.
ai_usage.when_not_to_use: For very large matrices (>1000×1000), consider iterative methods. For sparse matrices, use specialized sparse eigensolvers.

related_functions.name: phi_svd
related_functions.relation: SVD generalizes eigendecomposition to non-square matrices

related_functions.name: matrix_coherence
related_functions.relation: Uses Frobenius norm rather than eigenvalue spread
